title: COPY a file into image if it exists
body:
[https://stackoverflow.com/questions/31528384/conditional-copy-add-in-dockerfile](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/31528384/conditional-copy-add-in-dockerfile)

`COPY requiements.tx[t] /destination`

this command working well with docker, but when build with podman, it throw error "no such file or directory" while file requiements.txt not exists. podman not support the COPY pattern?

comments.body:
Hi @sparkwj ,

I just tried on latest upstream buildah and it worked fine for me.

```Containerfile
FROM alpine
RUN mkdir dest
COPY req.tx[t] /dest
RUN cat /dest/req.txt
```

Could you please let me know which version are you trying this with ?

spark@MacBook-Pro test % podman build .
```
STEP 1/4: FROM alpine
Resolved "alpine" as an alias (/etc/containers/registries.conf.d/000-shortnames.conf)
Trying to pull docker.io/library/alpine:latest...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob sha256:52f827f723504aa3325bb5a54247f0dc4b92bb72569525bc951532c4ef679bd4
Copying config sha256:7ad00e65ee25911881c06b97a3e562675d255e1265ba4abadd3e906d266c1dcc
Writing manifest to image destination
STEP 2/4: RUN mkdir dest
--> f0c006b17f14
STEP 3/4: COPY req.tx[t] /dest
Error: building at STEP "COPY req.tx[t] /dest": checking on sources under "/var/tmp/libpod_builder4102429835/build": Rel: can't make  relative to /var/tmp/libpod_builder4102429835/build; copier: stat: ["/req.tx[t]"]: no such file or directory
```
